title: Project
text:
library(tidyverse)

── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.1     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.3     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors

library(nycflights13)

title: Project
text:
Featured Projects

Customer Satisfaction Analysis
Data Cleaning with R
Google Analytics Reports
Dashboard Development

title: Project
text:
Row

Flights by Airline

flights %>%
  count(carrier) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(carrier, n), y = n, fill = carrier)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Flights by Airline",
    x = "Airline Carrier",
    y = "Flights"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()










Average Departure Delay

flights %>%
  group_by(carrier) %>%
  summarise(avg_delay = mean(dep_delay, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(carrier, avg_delay),
             y = avg_delay,
             fill = carrier)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Average Departure Delay",
    x = "Airline Carrier",
    y = "Average Delay (Minutes)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

title: Project
text:
Row

Flights by Month

flights %>%
  count(month) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(month), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Flights by Month",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of Flights"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()










Average Air Time by Month

flights %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(avg_air_time = mean(air_time, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(month), y = avg_air_time)) +
  geom_line(group = 1, color = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkred") +
  labs(
    title = "Average Air Time by Month",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Average Air Time (Minutes)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

title: Project
text:
Row

Dashboard Summary
This dashboard illustrates things taht i have learned through the NYC Flights 2013 dataset to demonstrate data visualization and dashboard design with Quarto. The charts highlight flight activity by airline, compare average departure delays, display monthly flight volume, and summarize average air time throughout the year. Dashboards like this provide an effective way to communicate insights and support data-driven decision-making. Additionally, data like the one provided in this package have allowed me to continue learning and growing as a very new Quarto and Rstudio user.

title: Home
text:
Welcome to my professional portfolio! I am a senior at California State Polytechnic University, Pomona, pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Business Administration with an emphasis in Marketing Management. My academic journey has allowed me to combine creativity with analytical thinking while developing technical skills in data analytics, visualization, and digital marketing.
This website showcases projects completed throughout my coursework and highlights my growth in marketing analytics, business intelligence, and data-driven decision making.




My educational journey has been shaped by diverse experiences. Before transferring to Cal Poly Pomona, I studied Architecture for two years at the University of San Francisco, where I developed a strong foundation in design thinking, creativity, and problem solving.
In Fall 2025, I had the opportunity to study abroad in Rome, Italy. Living and studying in another country broadened my global perspective, strengthened my adaptability, and reinforced my appreciation for different cultures and approaches to business.
As I begin my final year of undergraduate study, I am excited to continue developing my analytical, technical, and creative skills while preparing for a career in marketing and business analytics.




My goal is to build a career in marketing where I can combine creativity with data-driven decision making. I am particularly interested in marketing analytics, consumer insights, digital marketing, and business strategy.
Long-term, I hope to contribute to organizations that value innovation, collaboration, and diversity while continuing to grow professionally. I also aspire to one day launch my own business and apply both my creative vision and analytical expertise as an entrepreneur.





R Programming
Quarto
GitHub
Google Analytics
Data Visualization
Microsoft Excel
Microsoft Office Suite
Marketing Analytics
Business Reporting
Customer Service
Event Planning

Graduation: May 2027
